turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="g1" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#00b3ff"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#7cffd6"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="glow" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="4" result="b"/>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in="b"/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="512" fill="#07111c"/>
  <circle cx="210" cy="256" r="175" fill="none" stroke="#12324f" stroke-width="2"/>
  <circle cx="210" cy="256" r="145" fill="none" stroke="#0f2a40" stroke-width="2"/>

  <!-- speed streaks (left-to-right) -->
  <g stroke="#1a6fb0" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9">
    <line x1="120" y1="200" x2="320" y2="200" stroke-width="6"/>
    <line x1="110" y1="230" x2="330" y2="230" stroke-width="4" opacity="0.8"/>
    <line x1="115" y1="256" x2="335" y2="256" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.7"/>
    <line x1="110" y1="282" x2="330" y2="282" stroke-width="4" opacity="0.8"/>
    <line x1="120" y1="312" x2="320" y2="312" stroke-width="6" opacity="0.9"/>
  </g>

  <!-- central source (speaker/energy) -->
  <g filter="url(#glow)">
    <circle cx="205" cy="256" r="72" fill="#081b2c" stroke="#1f6fae" stroke-width="3"/>
    <circle cx="205" cy="256" r="50" fill="url(#g1)" opacity="0.15"/>
    <path d="M205 218 C230 230, 230 282, 205 294 C180 282, 180 230, 205 218 Z" fill="#00b3ff" opacity="0.25"/>
    <circle cx="205" cy="256" r="22" fill="#00b3ff" opacity="0.35"/>
  </g>

  <!-- sound waves (sonic) -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#74f7d5" stroke-width="4" filter="url(#glow)" opacity="0.95">
    <path d="M205 256 C250 220, 250 292, 205 256"/>
    <path d="M205 256 C280 196, 280 316, 205 256" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.85"/>
    <path d="M205 256 C315 165, 315 347, 205 256" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.7"/>
  </g>

  <!-- outer wave arcs -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="url(#g1)" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M175 256 C175 205, 235 205, 235 256 C235 307, 175 307, 175 256"/>
    <path d="M155 256 C155 176, 255 176, 255 256 C255 336, 155 336, 155 256" opacity="0.6"/>
  </g>

  <!-- arrow to suggest speed -->
  <g transform="translate(290 256)" filter="url(#glow)">
    <path d="M0 -18 L52 0 L0 18" fill="none" stroke="#7cffd6" stroke-width="6" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
    <line x1="2" y1="0" x2="86" y2="0" stroke="#7cffd6" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M74 -10 L94 0 L74 10" fill="none" stroke="#00b3ff" stroke-width="4" stroke-linejoin="round" opacity="0.9"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Designing a "sonic" graphic**

I'm thinking about how to create a visual for the word "sonic" without any text. I want to use stylized sound waves and a blue theme to capture that sonic vibe. It seems simple enough: I could have a central circle with three expanding wave arcs, maybe even include a lightning bolt to emphasize speed. I should depict speed lines and sound waves without using letters. I’ll produce an SVG at 512.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
